whenLight.$description: Resolves to 1 in light color schemes and 0 in dark color schemes.

whenDark.$description: Resolves to 1 in dark color schemes and 0 in light color schemes.

whenInverted.$description: Resolves to 1 in inverted color schemes and 0 in non-inverted color schemes.

schemeMultiplier.$description: Resolves to 1 in light color schemes and -1 in dark color schemes.

saturation.$description: Applies global saturation adjustments to all colors

density.$description: A scaling factor for density. Higher density means smaller, tighter spacing and size

baseSize.$description: Defines the base spacing unit used to derive spacing and layout tokens.

scaleBase.$description: The "base" number of the scale equation - a linear scalar multiplier. Set this to scale by some linear factor if scaleExponent is "1"

scaleExponentStep.$description: The exponent of the scale equation - the rate of exponential growth. `scaleBase` is raised to this exponent before being multiplied by the base spacing size. Set this to "1" to scale linearly by the `scaleBase` factor, or set it to a value greater than "1" to have exponential growth.

roundness.$description: Controls the roundness of corners. Larger roundness values also may affect padding.

lineWidth.$description: Controls the width of lines used for borders, etc.

spread.$description: Controls the spread size of all shadows.

blur.$description: Controls the blur scaling of all shadows.

baseFontSize.$description: Defines the root font size used to derive typography tokens.

weightStep.$description: The amount of font weight change between each typography level. This is multiplied by the level index to determine how much to adjust font weight at each level.

minWeight.$description: The minimum font weight to use in the typography scale.

maxWeight.$description: The maximum font weight to use in the typography scale.

baseWeight.$description: The base font weight from which to calculate typography levels. This is typically the "normal" font weight (e.g. 400), and level adjustments are applied as positive or negative offsets from this base.

lineHeightStep.$description: The amount of line height change between each typography level. This is multiplied by the level index to determine how much to adjust line height at each level.

minLineHeight.$description: The minimum line height to use in the typography scale.

maxLineHeight.$description: The maximum line height to use in the typography scale.

baseLineHeight.$description: The base line height from which to calculate typography levels. This is typically the line height used at the default typography level, and level adjustments are applied as positive or negative offsets from this base.

minFontSize.$description: The minimum font size to use in the typography scale.

maxFontSize.$description: The maximum font size to use in the typography scale.

fontSizeScaleBase.$description: The "base" number of the font size scale equation - a linear scalar multiplier. Set this to scale by some linear factor if sizeExponent is "1"

fontSizeScaleExponentStep.$description: The exponent of the font size scale equation - the rate of exponential growth. `fontSizeScaleBase` is raised to this exponent before being multiplied by the base font size. Set this to "1" to scale linearly by the `fontSizeScaleBase` factor, or set it to a value greater than "1" to have exponential growth.

letterSpacingStep.$description: The amount of letter spacing change between each typography level. This is multiplied by the level index to determine how much to adjust letter spacing at each level.

minLetterSpacing.$description: The minimum letter spacing to use in the typography scale.

maxLetterSpacing.$description: The maximum letter spacing to use in the typography scale.

baseLetterSpacing.$description: The base letter spacing from which to calculate typography levels. This is typically the letter spacing used at the default typography level, and level adjustments are applied as positive or negative offsets from this base.

darkModeWeightAdjustment.$description: An adjustment applied to font weight when in dark mode to compensate for the irradiation illusion. This is typically a negative value to reduce font weight in dark mode, but it can be positive if your theme has very light text and you want to increase weight in dark mode.

$root.$description: A convenient reference for the "md" spacing size

size.$description: The size of the font used in this typography level

lineHeight.$description: The line height of the font used in this typography level

letterSpacing.$description: The letter spacing of the font used in this typography level

$root.$description: A convenient reference for the "medium" duration

$root.$value: calc(calc(8px * pow(2, 1 * 0)) / 1) calc((calc(8px * pow(2, 1 * 1)) + 1 * calc(8px * pow(2, 1 * -1))) / 1)

roundness.$description: This token controls the overall roundness of actions and stacks with the root roundness token

radius.$description: This token captures the border-radius of actions, taking into account the overall roundness

$root.$value: calc(calc(8px * pow(2, 1 * 0)) / 1) calc((calc(8px * pow(2, 1 * 0)) + 1 * calc(8px * pow(2, 1 * -1))) / 1)

roundness.$description: This token controls the overall roundness of controls and stacks with the root roundness token

radius.$description: This token captures the border-radius of controls, taking into account the overall roundness

$root.$value: calc(calc(calc(8px * pow(2, 1 * 1)) / 1) * max(1, 1 * 1)) calc(calc(calc(8px * pow(2, 1 * 1)) / 1) * max(1, 1 * 1))

roundness.$description: This token controls the overall roundness of surfaces and stacks with the root roundness token

radius.$description: This token captures the border-radius of surfaces, taking into account the overall roundness

$root.$description: A convenient reference for the "md" spacing size
A convenient reference for the "md" border width

sm.$description: A hairline border width. Always >= 1px. If the global border width is small, this may be the same as "md"

lg.$description: A thicker border, good for emphasis

$root.$description: A convenient reference for the "md" border radius
A convenient reference for the "medium" duration

short.$description: A short, snappy duration, good for fast interactions and large animations

medium.$description: A medium duration, good for general use, fast enough for interactions

long.$description: A long, relaxed duration, good for slow interactions and animations

trueLightColor.$description: Either white or black, depending on the color scheme

trueHeavyColor.$description: Either white or black, depending on the color scheme

color.$description: Provides the default shadow color used when a shadow token does not supply its own color.

$root.$description: A convenient reference for the "mid" shade

paper.$description: An extremely light shade, good for backgrounds and surfaces

wash.$description: A very faint but slightly vibrant shade, good for backgrounds and surfaces

light.$description: A light shade, good for emphasized surfaces.

mid.$description: The main shade, good for primary actions and decoration.

heavy.$description: A heavy shade, good for text emphasis and accents.

ink.$description: A very dark but still slightly vibrant shade, good for text and high contrast elements.
